steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The parenthetical defines these features as impromptu deviations of performers when fidelity to an established musical score is not mandated. The word “impromptu” is decisive: it means spontaneous, done without prior preparation.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
The blank needs an adjective meaning spontaneous or improvised — the passage explicitly defines the features as impromptu deviations when no fixed score is required. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — meticulous: extremely careful and detailed; opposite of impromptu (which implies done without preparation). ✗
  • Choice B — inconspicuous: not easily noticed; the passage isn't about visibility but about spontaneity. ✗
  • Choice C — inevitable: certain to happen; impromptu deviations are by definition not inevitable — they're unpredictable. ✗
  • Choice D — extemporaneous: done on the spot without preparation; a near-synonym for impromptu. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
